Text: The Hunt for Aphorism

Description: Summon your little mermaid minion. Wishes to be a little part of your world.
DescriptionEnhanced: Legends pertaining to mermaids are common in the Corvos region, foremost among them the tale of a mermaid princess who falls in love with a man. Perhaps influenced by said tale, this diminutive entity born of an arcane pattern in an illuminated manuscript has chosen to accompany you on your journeys.
SpecialActionDescription:
Delivers an attack with a potency of 60 to all enemies within range while reducing movement speed by 70%.
**Duration:** 10s

Description: Summon your magic lamp minion. Phenomenal cosmic powers in an itty-bitty living space.
DescriptionEnhanced: Manifesting from an arcane pattern in an illuminated manuscript, this oil lamp appears to be home to a genie. Unlike the lamp-dwelling spirit of Corvosi legend that grants wishes, however, this one is hesitant to show itself, perhaps needing more time to decide whether you are a worthy master.
Tooltip:

Text: The Merchant's Tale
Another Merchant's Tale
Variant Dungeon (Advanced)

Description:
Your latest odd job commission brings you together with one Y'nazqha, a gleaner who finds herself in possession of an unusual book inherited from her father and mentor. It contains a rendition of *The Merchant's Tale*, a fable passed down through generations in Corvos. While the beautifully illuminated pages of the manuscript set it apart from common storybooks, its most singular feature is the enchantment which can pull the very mind of its readers into the story.

And so it is you are thrust into the role of bodyguard for Y'nazqha, who becomes the eponymous merchant while journeying within the tale. Together you must travel through a world steeped in folklore and find a treasure of immeasurable worth─treasure grand enough to stir even the most hardened heart.

*The Merchant's Tale* depicts a Corvos from before the Garlean occupation, made even more wondrous by the flourishes of countless storytellers throughout the ages. As you relate the details of your time within its pages to Osmon, he continues the timeless storytelling tradition by adding his own embellishments to an already fantastic fable, and you feel yourself drawn back in to the beautiful but increasingly dangerous world of folklore...

The dutiful mammet Memolivia listens to your every word with what you can only guess is rapt attention, speedily writing down the details in incomprehensible shorthand. It then begins trimming the text further until naught remains but concise descriptions of the fiercest battles you encountered in *The Merchant's Tale*. As you read the record over, you find that without the trappings of that wondrous story to soften them, the foes you faced were terrifying beyond your wildest imaginings...

Text: Which way should we go?
Let's follow the fruit.
I have a good feeling about the other way.
The seaweed seems much denser on the right side. Rich waters might hold even richer treasures...
Which way should we go?
The seaweed won't steer us wrong.
I fancy a stroll along the beach.
Our first fork in the road. Where should we go?
Surprise me!
Let's take the back street.
Let's head to the market.
I'd like to see the grand gate.
These apples were grown by a caretaker's hand. Beyond the orchard we might find a settlement!
On one side, tantalizing mushrooms. On the other...floating rocks?
Which way should we go?
Let the mushrooms guide us true!
Floating rocks warrant a closer look.
Many thanks for the help!
Apologies, but I can't open shop just yet...

Text[0]: How will the story unfold this time? I simply can't wait to find out!
I never would have guessed that my dream was actually symbolizing discovery and hope! Thanks to your efforts, now all may enjoy the festivities of Hatching–tide.
So the golden eggs in my dream represented a golden age of alchemy... We could never have made Hatching-tide possible without you, Player. Thank you so much!
You did a marvelous job trimming down those leafkin. Thank you, Player!
I've never seen a trimming quite so thorough. Or stylish! You really showed us the true spirit of an adventurer.
Oh! Fancy meeting you here. Finding many eggs?
Oh! It's good to see you, Player. Finding many eggs?
Text[1]: We're here as envoys. The Elder Seedseer suggested we take part in these festivities when we had a moment, and I'm glad we did!
The Elder Seedseer recommended we participate in these festivities since we're visiting as envoys. And I'm glad we did!
Text[2]: I'm going to collect as many of these amazing eggs as I can, and take them home as presents for Koana and Gulool Ja!
Koana and Gulool Ja are going to *love* these cute little eggs. I won't leave till I've collected as many as I can!

Text[0]: Player! I never thought I'd be speaking with you in Eorzea...
Player! What a delight to be speaking with you in Eorzea...
Text[1]: Alphinaud and the others have spoken with such love for these lands that I longed to see them for myself. I hope we may come to forge a lasting friendship.
My hope is that our nations may forge a deep and lasting friendship, so I am glad to have the opportunity to visit.
Text[2]: Everyone here is so kind. In fact, I was gifted this outfit by a lady named Jihli. <chuckle> Do you think people back home would be surprised to find me dressed so?
Gridania is beautiful, and everyone here is so kind. In fact, I was gifted this outfit by a lady named Jihli. <chuckle> I have half a mind to surprise everyone back home by returning in this guise!

Text[0]: Player! I had hoped I might run into you. I bet you're surprised to see me here of all places!
I had hoped I might run into you! Surprised to see me here?
Text[1]: Given the changes happening for the Arcadion, I thought I should broaden my horizons beyond the dome. A festival like this, where everyone can participate, is giving me ideas...
After watching you revolutionize the Arcadion, I wanted to broaden my own horizons. See things I've never seen, try things I've never tried. And it turns out a rough-and-tumble egg hunt is great fun!
Text[2]: Anyway, stop by the gym the next time you're in Alexandria. We're always happy to see you!
Anyway, stop by the gym the next time you're in Alexandria. We're always happy to have you!

Text[0]: I'm so used to the construction of Solution Nine that these verdant environs are peculiar to behold. The egg hunt was great fun as well.
There's so much green here... This place is a world apart from Solution Nine.
Text[1]: At least, it was until my sisters and I all laid claim to the same egg... When I thought it might come to blows, however, I eventually backed down.
The egg hunt was fun, too─at least, until my sisters laid claim to the same egg. I thought it might come to blows, so I said that *I* wanted it, and they both backed down.

Text[0]: Never did I imagine I would embark upon a journey beyond the dome. I owe this opportunity to you.
Never did I imagine I would journey beyond the dome. So many opportunities I owe to you.
Text[1]: The opportunity to take joy in these festivities, collect eggs with my sisters, exchange trinkets for prizes... I should take something back with me, to remember this time by.
The opportunity to relax with my sisters, to collect eggs and exchange them for whimsical little prizes... Opportunities that I will treasure for the rest of my days.

Text[0]: Well, if this festival isn't just the most darling little event I've ever seen!
Well, if this festival isn't the most darling little event I've ever seen!
Text[1]: I would have brought my broadcasting equipment had I known! Eggs, sunglasses...which of these adorable accoutrements would go well with my costume, do you think?
I would have brought my broadcasting equipment had I known! I'll just have to incorporate some of these *adorable* accessories into my stage costume instead.

Text[0]: I'm egg-static that my delightful darlings have been represented in these spectacles! I myself am sporting a pair as we speak. Beneath my festive guise, of course.
I'm *egg*static that my delightful darlings were replicated in these spectacles! I'm sporting a pair myself, you know. Beneath my festive guise.

Text[2]: I'm egg-static that my delightful darlings have been represented in these spectacles! I myself am sporting a pair as we speak. Beneath my festive guise, of course.
I'm *egg*static that my delightful darlings were replicated in these spectacles! I'm sporting a pair myself, you know. Beneath my festive guise.

Text[0]: Hmhmhm... I eagerly await the advent of tonberry spectacles. The realm may not yet be ready for their splendor, but in time, all shall come to realize the glory that is tonberry! <heavy breathing>
Hmhmhm... I eagerly await the advent of tonberry spectacles. The realm may not yet be prepared for their splendor, but in time, all shall come to realize the glory that is tonberry! <heavy breathing>
Good evening, milady! I hear you are to thank for the weeding that has allowed this humble enthusiast to collect his eggs in peace and safety. <snort>
Good evening, milady! I believe *you* are to thank for the weeding that has allowed this humble enthusiast to collect his eggs in peace and safety. <snort>

Speaking of passion, how does the egg hunting arena fare? If you venture out for another trim, Herberin the Perfervid would be honored to accompany you!
Speaking of passion, how does the arena fare? Fancy another trim? Another slice? Another delightful delivery of luscious leafkin? Hmm, hmm!
The maiden ignores you. It seems her dialogue for this portion of the tale has been exhausted.

Text[0]: Truth be told, I didn't expect to meet an honest-to-gods hero this day. Erenville's stories about you beggar belief, but now I'm beginning to think they're true after all.
How fare your journeys into the book's pages? Nazqha has a free spirit, but you may need to rein in her enthusiasm from time to time.
It's good to see you. Have you witnessed the end of the tale?
Nazqha has a kind heart and a sharp mind, but also a shockingly reckless streak.
Text[1]: Surely you've witnessed this side of her already. If something catches her fancy, she won't hesitate to give it a taste.
Text[2]: Still, she is well-read, so I think she knows when something shouldn't be in her stomach. Otherwise, she wouldn't have come this far as a gleaner.

Text[0]: Grrr, if it wasn't for that meddling, third-rate...
Sheesh, Player. I work fast, but not that fast. Come back another day, will ya?
Text[1]: Oh, before you go... Do you remember when I said I felt eyes on me? Well, I still feel like I'm being watched. Maybe I *do* have some addle-headed admirer after all.

Text[0]: I'll get in touch with Nazqha, so I'd be grateful if you waited for her at Archons' Design. Should be the perfect place for a chat.
Worry not, for the manuscript is in our safekeeping. I hope your time within its pages was as thrilling as your other adventures.
I'm relieved that you and Nazqha came back unharmed, and I can tell Fyrgeim feels the same. It'll be good to rest a while without having to fret over your safety.
My NaMe Is MeMoLiViA, aNd My DuTy Is To ReCoRd YoUr AdVeNtUrEs As CoNcIsElY aS pOsSiBlE!
I'm told you have a penchant for Triple Triad. I myself have amassed something of a deck in my travels, if you care for a challenge.
Well, well. Seems my many matches on the road have given me the edge.
Text[1]: I plan to stay here a while, so don't be a stranger. I'm sure you'll want your revenge soon enough.

Text[0]: Gods, what a play! I admit defeat...though I've not had that much fun in a long time.
Text[1]: I ought to pick up a few more cards when next my duties call me to the road... But until then, I'm stuck with this deck─and I'd be keen to rematch you whenever you've the time.

Text[0]: A draw, eh? Not the outcome I wanted, but I'll let it be, if you're satisfied. Come back again when you've got time to kill. I'll be here.
Nazqha is already making preparations for her next journey. It's one job after another with her, but that simply shows how sought after her skills and knowledge are.

Text: By participating in Sprig Cleaning, you will help clear away pests that are impeding Hatching-tide festivities.
By participating in Sprig Cleaning, you will use special actions to help clear away pests that are impeding Hatching-tide festivities.

■基本情報
スプリグ・クリーニングは、ソロでのみ遊べるコンテンツです。
コンテンツの制限時間は3分です。

ゲーム中は、専用アクションを駆使して魔物を刈り、
より多くのスコアを獲得することが目的となります。

■報酬
ゲーム中に獲得したスコアの量に応じて、
「スペシャル・トリコエッグ」を入手することできます。

スペシャル・トリコエッグは、
ミィ・ケット野外音楽堂の「エッグドリーマー」に渡すことで、
装備品やアイテムと交換できます。

■Basic Rules
Sprig Cleaning is a solo duty with a three-minute time limit.

Use special actions to trim back the menacing weeds you encounter and aim for the highest score possible.

■Rewards
Players will receive special brilliant eggs proportionate to their score. These rewards can be exchanged for gear and items by speaking with the Dreamer in Mih Khetto's Amphitheatre.
ゲーム中、特殊な魔物「フシギナ・トラップ」が出現することがあります。
You may encounter transcendental traps during Sprig Cleaning.

このフシギナ・トラップを刈ると、
以下の特殊な効果がランダムで発生します。

・草刈り魂上昇
「草刈り魂」が大幅に上昇します。

・アルコンエッグ発見率上昇
アルコンエッグを見つけやすくなります。

・アルコンエッグ【金】率上昇
見つけたアルコンエッグが、必ずアルコンエッグ【金】になります。

Trimming these leafkin will randomly apply one of the following benefits:

・A significant increase to your cleaning gauge

・Status Effect: Enhanced Egg Acuity
　Improves your chances of discovering Archon eggs.

・Status Effect: Golden Eye
　Ensures all Archon eggs discovered are gold.

Message:
I pray this letter finds its way into the hands of a kind-hearted soul, who shall bear word of the ruin encroaching upon my home to a warrior of courage.
The Dandan terrorizes our village, raising waves to topple our houses and lashing its tail to sink our ships. We are now cut off from our neighbors, and our reserves have dwindled to naught. Before long, none shall remain.
If only we could join hands with the beautiful maidens who dwell in the sea...yet now even they are beyond our reach.

We nabbed some geezer from the town and made him spill his guts. It's just like you said, Boss. Deep in this canyon, there's some hidden place that hardly a soul knows about.
I reckon we'll find treasure the likes we've never dreamed of! Me and a few of the lads went to get eyes on it, but we stepped right into the nest of some monstrous bird. Barely made it out with our lives.
We've scrounged together every weapon and bomb we could, and we're standing by until you say the word. After we've cut and blasted our way through, we'll be richer than kings!

How many seasons have come and gone since I set foot in this land, where the rules of gravity are mere suggestions? How many times have I swung my sword—each flourish bringing me one step closer to mastering the Unyielding Blade, honing the aether within my weapon to a fine edge?
Yet even as my body is consumed by training, my mind turns only to my dear friend, bound still to the sea. To destroy the vile thing which threatens her and hers, my bladework must be without equal.
When I have reached the heights I must, I shall come for you. I will stand upon those shores of our home once more and cry out: “I am returned!”

Text: <sigh> What a chore. Must you soil the floor of my paradise?
Put those tattered threads to use!
What persistent pests... Come now, you worthless rugs, shake off that dirt and do something!
I've had quite enough of you! I shall like you better set aflame!
Your prancing and capering sickens me! Begone from my sight!
I tire of this. Go on, you frayed mats─smother the life out of them.
Greedy bird! I only stole a few snacks!
Hahaha, too slow! That food was delicious, by the way! Until next time!
Gyah! Gyaaah!
You got in the way of our fun, and now it's time to pay the price!
It helped us! Perhaps as a show of thanks for before?
Stop struggling! You wear my patience dangerously thin!
You'll soon be a pile of ash!
●未使用
Where have I seen this before...? Careful─it appears to be in a foul mood!
What? It disappeared? There *was* a ball of dirt over there, right?
Wait, the balls are still here, we just can't see them! Remember their position before they turn invisible!
It must still be here somewhere! Look carefully for traces of its passage!
<cough> <wheeze> ...There you are! Reckoned we'd sit back lickin' our wounds, eh?
Time for payback! You'll regret crossin' us!
Wh-What's that shakin'!? R-Run! This revenge ain't worth dyin' for!
They've run out of eyeshot. I suppose every story needs its petty villains...
We slaved for days to make those bombs! Good thing we've got other ways to make you suffer!
Godsdammit, why didn't you bring more!? Get the hells out of here!
It can turn the seeds invisible too!? Remember where they are!
Come, join us. Lend your voice to our chorus!
Let our song ring ever louder!
More minions! Listen to her voice carefully, Player!
These creatures are different than before... Be wary of their attacks!
歌いましょう、美しき愛が届くように……。
They're being guided by her singing. Watch your back!
Dance with me. Let the beat of our hearts guide our steps.
She's swimming around again. Don't get caught in her wake!
Now we offer ourselves to the sea. Hope overflowing coalesces into a wish come true...
The orbs are changing─keep an eye on them!
Let us shout our prayers to the heavens, and they may be answered.
Watch your step! Dense aether yet lingers!
You can but walk the path fate sets before you.
Surrender yourself to the ocean's embrace.
Here comes the wave again! Watch the water carefully!
Be buried under the weight of countless unanswered wishes...
Let us treasure this bond, ere it vanishes like so much foam upon the tide...
Our revel shall not end until our prayers are answered.
Do my words not reach you? I ask only that you stay by my side...
Now, shall we depart? Into the black, inky depths!

ああ……愛しき人……
あなたも、共には来てくれないのね……。
Clear *The Merchant's Tale*
Clear *The Merchant's Tale*
Defeat the Pari of Plenty
Defeat Darya the Sea-maid
Defeat the Lone Swordmaster
Clear *The Merchant's Tale*
Player, look! That woman is under attack!
I am returned!
A beautiful place, to be sure, but sprawling. Perhaps the locals can help us catch the scent of treasure, eh?
This must be the heart of the city. We've many roads to choose from. Do any pique your interest?
Ah, I see we think alike! Let us proceed.
Oh? *That* way? Well, I'm not one to gainsay an adventurer's intuition, so lead the way!
You're fine with me making the choice? Well, my gleaner's intuition is pointing me...this way!
●最初に訪れたのは“住宅街”……
●最初に訪れたのは“市場”……
●最初に訪れたのは“正門通り”……
Beasts, here!? It looks like they're attacking someone─we must help, Player!
Whenever I draw near the rocks, the fur on my tail stands on end! What could be causing that?
A lot of good invisibility did that beast! Watch for shaking flowers─we don't want another surprise!
I'm...I'm unharmed, thanks to you. Just who are you, anyways?
Hmm... Perhaps you'll find what you seek in the plains to the west. There lies an old abandoned palace, said to house a veritable mountain of treasure.
What could be in an abandoned palace but abandoned treasure? Let our search begin in earnest!
Shall we travel to the northern valley, Player? As insufferable as thieves are, they often hoard treasure!
There's nothing like the gentle caress of the wind! And that smell...something sweet is in the air!
So that was the scent of apples! I thought so. The orchard keeps going in that direction, it seems.
If there's a settlement nearby, someone might be able to point us towards the nearest treasure.
I see your thinking: where there's fewer people, there's more hidden treasure to be found!
I, for one, could not put a price on a treasure such as this! Let us bring it to the maiden!
Exactly the outcome I expected! Even an invisible enemy is no match for your cunning, Player!
This has to be of great value─value immeasurable, one might say! Let us deliver this to the maiden!
I must say, that was a grand display! If that genie wasn't trying to kill us, I would certainly watch it again!
A new path! It seems the rocks moved... Hmm. Were they imbued with some unseen power?
The horizon stretches as far as the eye can see! Oh, is that...? Why, I think it's the palace!
Player!? Are you... Oh, can I get down from over there!?
Here we are! I expect we'll be accosted soon enough, so be careful!
What divine flavor! I scarcely need to chew, and the aroma overwhelms the senses!
I can tell you're curious too. My intuition agrees─follow the mushrooms!
Are you alright, Player!? The railing broke!
Are you hurt? To think we would find a path here of all places...
The rock is floating in the air! It looked as though some kind of power was being channeled into it...
I haven't seen anything quite like this... I believe we have no choice but to get a closer look!
It seems with that, all the rocks in the area are floating. I wonder if anything else changed...?
That must be one of the legendary swordmasters... Even I can sense she is a formidable foe. But not as formidable as *you*, I'm sure!
That was quite the fight! Despite appearances, her strength was terrifying to behold.
It's one of the carpets...she just left it here. Now that I see it up close, 'tis plain it was woven by a master.
What's the matter?
The damned wheels came right off... Suppose I'll be carrying my haul the rest of the way.
Well, we can help with that. If we split the load, we should be able to make it in one go!
Say, if I asked you to imagine an object of immeasurable worth, what would come to mind?
Looking to make some coin, eh? Hmm. Silk, gemstones, spices. All those would fetch a high price.
Come to think of it, I heard sea-folk live in the cove to the east. If anyone had a treasure of singular worth, it would be them.
Thank you for that precious morsel of information, my good man! I believe we have our next destination, Player!
Ah, could that be? The Anchorite at Corvos? I read that the rocky formations on both sides of the strait are actually the ankles of a massive statue!
I think I'll stay well away from the open sea for some time... Well, let's get to land, yes?
Not a single soul to be found...but plainly, this place once thrived.
That was─how should I put it?─quite the cheerful adversary. Perhaps it was welcoming us, in its own way...
Wait... That carpet. Is it not the selfsame design we saw in the maiden's room?
Indeed, it is edible! This unique flavor would make for a perfect spice in certain dishes.
Indeed, these waters may hold treasures all their own! I look forward to the sights which await us.
Indeed, I see shells and pearls glittering among the sands... All right, lead the way!
There's a plate with a matching pattern, too. It's quite dirty, but I think we should take it back with us.
The marks of civilization are plain to see. Was this once a place of worship?
Are you all right!? To think they would be so bold as to attack on a well-traveled avenue...

あれが噂の人魚でしょうか……？
友好的とは限りません、ご注意ください！
We made it! Although the sea-maid tried to kill us, something about her seemed...mournful.
Look, she dropped this gem. Its glow is entrancing...'tis probably best not to stare too long.
You saved my life, truly. Those thieves but recently began to infest the valley to the north.
Did it answer the shell's call? What an adorable thing─and it's colored just like the cove!
Oh, most joyful of days! At last, voices from the land call out to me again!
Pretty creatures, allow me to guide you. What you seek lies at the end of this path.
Yet you must tread carefully. It passes through the territory of a dreadful beast...where my voice will not reach you.
Why, she hasn't even bothered to get up! Does she consider us beneath her, or is she just lazy?
It seems the carpets do her bidding... Heavens forfend she lift a finger herself!
Is that the bird from before!?
Now make yourselves useful, you old rags. You *do* know what I mean, yes?
A spirit come to join the fray? Good, good. The lighter my burden, the better.
Is that a magic lamp I see!? Just how long was it waiting for us?
Welcome, welcome! The guests of honor are here at last!
The curtain rises on the big show! Dare not look away, or else!
Let us end on a high note! An *explosive* note!
Now we turn up the heat! Do make sure not to get burned!
Oh ho, you really know how to put on a show! But I can't let my own guests upstage me!
Splendid! Look how they dart about! Be aware that I take no responsibility in case of grievous injury!
That was only the opening act! Keep your eyes peeled─you won't want to miss this next part!
Keep your wits about you and your eyes sharp! An opening will present itself!
What do we have here? Surprise guests! This will be one for the ages!
Another foe!? And here I thought the water would be a boon...
Now for the fun part! Be dazzled! Be amazed! Be hypnotized!
How breathtaking! And...dangerous! Yes, dangerous! Stay focused!
That was like seeing two artists at work! My jaw was clenched the whole time...but in the end, you proved the mightier!
Why, it's the swordmaster's blade. The edge is flawless, and the decorations are exquisite!
Take up your weapon. I pray you can provide some measure of entertainment before you die.
Death comes from all directions!
Run if you dare! Doom shall find you all the same!
Know this: none can escape their fate.
Whence shall my blade strike? Look well!
Bear witness to the Unyielding Blade!
Do you truly believe you can run from me?
Hah hah, good, good! Perhaps you shall not disappoint after all!
Let us see how attuned you are to the flow of battle!
Do not dare perish before witnessing my full strength!
Only a true master can call the earth itself her ally!
Not so fast!
Behold the depths of my art!
Come now, you can do better than that!
...Tch, you have much to learn. Sadly, the dead do not learn. They sleep.
Now for the part no one expects─the encore! Can you keep up the energy for just a little longer?
Bang! Boom! And a blast for good measure!
The show must go on!
Have you ever had so much fun!?
What a show! But it can always be better!
You're still standing? Ugh... I was hoping this would be over by now.
Blades of aether! Don't let them pierce you!
Falling stones! Watch your head!
The magnetic fields are wildly unstable... Perhaps our foe uses them against us.
The blades of aether changed trajectory!? I see now why the Unyielding Blade was so feared...
I must say, I certainly couldn't measure the value of this. Could you? No? I believe that means we should deliver it to the maiden!
Waves are coming from everywhere at once! Don't let them knock you over!
I'm but a humble merchant, and this is my trusty bodyguard. We are searching for treasure said to be of value immeasurable.
Player, listen. Did you hear a sound just now?
Could this have been her home...?
This fighting style...sword strikes imbued with aether. Could this be one of the swordmasters that were snuffed out by the Empire!?
From where shall death take you? I shall grant you the mercy of foresight.
Crates delivered

Description: Summon forth your genie of the lamp. Not the actual being of legend so cannot grant wishes─unless your wish is to be carried somewhere.
DescriptionEnhanced: In the region of Corvos, there exists a legend of a lamp-dwelling spirit that grants wishes to its master. As this particular entity is an imitation later brought forth by a sorcerer, it is far from the omnipotent being described in tales, but in accordance with its creator's design, it will dutifully carry you wherever you wish to go.
Tooltip:
What child wouldn't have
wanted a friend like that?
   - Beruru

Try catching us now, ya bastards!

They have carpets.
*Flying* carpets!

Text: A nap sounds heavenly...
This ordeal is still going?
Sigh...
This is taking too long...
Oh? You're still here?
Ah, I see we're going this way now.
Do try and make the ride less bumpy this time.
Ugh. Their voices grate on the ears.
Eeek!
<chirp> <screech>
Big dumb bird! And slow, oh so slow!
Grrr... Grraaah!
Oh, the noise... Can't we just have silence?
Ouch!
That hurts!
How dare you!
You will rue this day!
Play with us again sometime!
Get away, you blubbering bird! Stop nipping at my heels!
Another nuisance... Well, go on. Kill each other.
Time to wake up, you dusty things.
Even they tire of your company...
Grooo... Grooh ♪
Now, let our adventure begin!
My senses tell me...
The colors are breathtaking!
Are you hurt?
An abandoned palace? Why, that sounds perfect! Thank you, kind sir.
It's the least I could offer. Safe travels.
That tree...
If you don't mind!
This is no time to be staring at apples trees, Nazqha!
This tale has no shortage of danger!
Get your weapon ready!
I leave the combat to you!
I didn't expect to see bombs here...
Do you see that, Player!? It's almost as if...it's beckoning us forward.
What wondrous sights await us, I wonder?
Are those...grapes!?
Mmm... They are! Slightly bitter, but delightfully sweet!
You know, we have selectively bred grapes in Labyrinthos.
Oh, and here I was hoping for a feast!
Those grapes almost took a bite out of me!
Now how do we get down there...
What have we here...?
Here I go!
I made it! And I didn't even trip! ...Hm?
Ah, I saw these flowers in a field guide! I believe they're used to make honey.
I've seen this grass in a field guide...
Is that a bird's nest? Those look like chicks.
Hehehe! I'll be taking this!
Hahaha!
It stole their food!
See you never!
Ouch!
Ungh... You'll regret this!
<screech> <screech>
It sounds grateful that we protected its chicks!
<chirp>
<squeak> <squeak>
<squeak> <squeak> <squeak>
<squeak> <chirp>
<chirp> <chirp>
<chirp>!
<squeak>
<squeak> ...
<screech>
<squeak>! <squeak>!
<screech>! <screech>!
<chirp>! <chirp>!
I think I've seen those in a picture book somewhere...
Careful, or you 'll be blown away!
It's beautiful! I wonder what lies behind the other entrances.
Although our goal is in sight, we mustn't let our guard down!
Let us keep moving forward!
Friends of the spirit we encountered before?
I wonder if such a palace actually existed long ago.
All piled up without a care...yet they all seem so immensely valuable.
There's treasure here, I can feel it!
I could not have asked for a more dependable bodyguard!
I've never seen such elaborate weaving!
Look, over there. Someone's in trouble.
This isn't good...
This may help us progress the story─let's give this merchant a hand!
Lift with the knees...
My thanks. The shop is just over this way.
That got the blood flowing...
Your wares are all here!
That should be everything!
Sea-folk...could it truly be?
That beast...I know it!
Those claws...they're so big!
Into the cave!
Brighter than I expected. And much less dreary!
That's a lot of bombs!
Look! The blast knocked the wall loose!
Do you think that water is from the ocean?
Ugh... Rubble flew into my eyes.
There's something there!
It appears we can get out from over there!
Oh my! This beach is gorgeous!
Even the shells sparkle!
I imagine they would prove quite popular if used for pearl inlay.
Do such shells actually exist? I shall have to look into it when I return.
Ah, but this is no time for such musings!
I've never seen such coloring before...
Don't slip, Player.
The coral shines like nothing else...
<slurp>
Even the goobbues have fantastic colors!
I'm glad we weren't pulled into that goobbue's gullet.
Look at those rocks. Something's strange about them...
I think we can go this way!
Some sort of stage...?
You played your part as bodyguard perfectly. Thank you, Player!
What gave rise to this color, do you think?
That pearl is larger than me! One moment, please...
<gulp>
What causes such growth in these waters?
Impressive as always, Player!
Huh!? Did that sound come from that massive shell?
May we meet again.
Well then, let us press on!
Urgh... The pleasant scent of ocean and rot...
Perhaps the sea-folk are nearby.
The figures were carven by a skilled hand, indeed...
They lit up!
By the Twelve, it's beautiful!
Hmm? What's that sound?
Does that voice belong to one of the sea-folk?
What!? It appeared from nowhere!
I can't hear her anymore...
Well, we've no choice but to move forward, right?
I'm...I'm safe, thanks to you. Who are you?
No more than a humble merchant in search of treasure.
It's a nanka! They can be found here as well, it seems.
...'Tis a bitter taste.
*Very* bitter.
Let us return. I can only hope this will offer her some comfort.
Ah, look! Could those be...
I knew it! This singular blend of bitter and sweet...
You know, we grow these in Labyrinthos as well─ Player, watch out!
Oh my, they taste sweet as the summer sun! And that smell─absolutely heavenly!
Ugh! Bitter! I suppose these could be used treat headaches...
<gasp>
It would take a lifetime to count all this!
The brilliant blue, the dazzling white... It's like something out of a picture book!
Have you ever seen such a gate!?
Hmm...?
Nooo!
Hehe, dressed up like a noble, are ya?
Suppose that means you've got riches like a noble. Why not share the wealth?
You think you're better than us!?
Don't get between us and our spoils!
We-We've seen the error of our ways!
Just spare our lives!
Thieves, eh? The classic antagonists!
What a strange feeling...
Thieves!?
Excellent work!
The sprites are effervescent! This place must be suffuse with aether.
That looks slippery...
It's trampling the flowers!
Watch your step!
Don't stand too close to the edge...
Oh my, the stones are floating! I wonder what keeps them aloft...
I can scarcely resist the urge to stand atop them.
I sense no magicks at work... What could be behind this?
What could those adorable creatures be?
What have we here? Mushrooms?
Ungh... I can barely move!
It seems no path is without obstacles!
Is that the scent of treasure...?
I suppose I was mistaken... Nothing appears worth taking.
Not the easiest place to fight through, was it?
Player, Player! Am I...*floating*!?
What a peculiar sensation!
I don't know where to step, nor where to hide!
Even without solid ground under your feet, you made quick work of them. Of course, I never doubted you!
I feel like I'm still floating...
Some sort of rock beast, I presume?
We've come quite high, haven't we?
Since we both have all of our limbs, I would say that was a job well done. Thank you, Player!
Eyaaah! Apologies, but combat is not my specialty!
Stay away!
Take this!
Have a taste!
Hah hah!
I'll blast you to bits!
How dare you!
Grrr! Now you've made me mad!
Lalalalala.♪
Abracadabra!
I am Nanaki, Watcher of Cosmo Canyon!
You listenin' to a word we're sayin'!?
Hand it over!
Be well, friends.
Tread carefully...very carefully.
Are golems affected by this strange magnetism too?
Its surface was hard as rock, but it moved nimbly enough...
Ah hah, do you feel that? Gravity! I can step surely now.
I must wonder as to the nature of the rocks... Perhaps their magnetic fields have been disrupted?
Unfortunately, I hardly think floating rocks could be considered treasure...
My heart is still pounding! Wait, is this...?
This is the last place I expected to run into a person...
That...doesn't look good!
<flop>
What are those!?
Even the beasts have a sweet scent about them!
Those appeared to be naught but seedlings... I shudder to think what they might grow into.
We should get to a safe distance before they explode.
May priceless riches await!
And the story unfurls further!
Now I must look twice at every rock I see...
Are those...flowers?
It is...
I see. Just how much would we need for *immeasurable* profit...?

Name: Rukhkh
Description: This chilling cloudkin is an antagonizing force in *The Merchant's Tale*, a fable of Corvosi origin. Though details vary by region and generation, all versions depict a predator that cloaks itself in cold fog, snatching up seafaring ships in its great talons and dropping them to their doom.

Name: Genie of the Lamp
Description: Full many Corvosi legends are centered on mystical artifacts, the magic lamp foremost among them. Give it a firm rub, the story goes, to draw forth a faerie with the ability to grant wishes.

Name: Pari of Plenty
Description: Ancient Corvosi legend tells of a king who, upon defeating this faerie of fire, demanded the production of a flying carpet. This depiction was later popularized in fables like *The Merchant's Tale*, from which she is now most famously known.

Name: Darya the Sea-maid
Description: Some say that Corvos harbors a hidden community of maritime folk half-covered with scales. Lovers of the sea are welcomed warmly, but woe betide any who dare to pollute it. This depiction also features in certain editions of *The Merchant's Tale*.

Name: Lone Swordmaster
Description: Expert practitioners of the Unyielding Blade, the traditional Corvosi style of swordplay, are formally known as swordmasters. It is said the last man to hold the title was taken captive following a failed rebellion against the Garlean Empire and ultimately slain by Zenos yae Galvus.

Name: Deadly Dandan
Description: For generations have the seafaring peoples of Corvos passed down the legend of this wicked wavekin, embodiment of all things great and terrible. Yet so tremendous was its perceived power that it was once summoned as a primal in a desperate bid to defeat the Garlean Empire.

Name: A Land of Abundance
Description:
*The merchant pressed on toward the timeworn palace, the wind against his face bearing the rich scent of nature.*
*The Merchant's Tale* is a well-known fable passed down in Corvos, located in southern Ilsabard. Though attributed to no single author, it has for centuries been a beloved favorite of children in the Near East. Due to its long history, particulars of the story vary greatly depending on who is telling it and when.
Prior to our most recent journey into the book's pages, I perused several editions of *The Merchant's Tale* housed in the archives of Noumenon and found multiple variations of the narrative's development and conclusion. Yet despite their differences, they all shared several connecting threads. Namely, the main plot is driven by a young merchant's search for a treasure to win the heart of his beloved. Moreover, they all describe the setting─Corvos itself─as a breathtaking locale where emerald greenery stretches as far as the eye can see. This is not far from reality, as Corvos is renowned even now for its warm, pleasant clime and rich soil which bestows plentiful harvests.
Indeed, the palace we encountered on this foray was surrounded by abundance, yet the structure itself was seemingly forgotten by time. Ruins oft make an appearance in various editions of the story in the form of abandoned palaces, villages razed by wild beasts, and so on. Perhaps the aim of such elements is to impress upon readers the inexorable power of nature and its creatures.

Name: A Carpet Soars
Description:
*Woven with golden thread, the carpet glided through the night sky, silent and shimmering like a falling star.*
Although the palace was laden with treasure, we chose to bring back a magicked carpet. We witnessed our foe using it to fly hither and thither, and it seemed to obey the commands of its master absolutely. As appealing as a mountain of gems might be, one cannot put a price on the ability to soar through the skies.
The origins of such carpets are widely known in the Near East. As the maiden told us, it is said an ancient Corvosi king entrapped a pari and forced her to create them. In fact, one scholar of folklore found evidence that this legend predates *The Merchant's Tale*. Perhaps the fable weaves together several others which circulated through the region during the same time period, meaning that the illuminated manuscript is a rich tapestry of Corvosi tradition and culture. Its value as a scholarly and historic document cannot be overstated.
As it happens, there have been attempts to recreate the fabled carpets through magical means, with some success being seen in recent times. Oh, how simple life would be if, like the pari, one could simply drift off and be carried in the direction of one's whims. Yet I suppose that would rob one of the pleasures of the journey itself. For now, I am content to travel by my own two feet.

Name: A Foe of Flame
Description:
*The pari rose to her feet, and in an instant all was wreathed in flames.*
Our last foe was almost comically slothful, but perhaps I would be too if I had flying carpets at my beck and call. Still, she put up a fierce fight, and I shudder to think what might have happened if she took us more seriously. Given how effortlessly she called a rain of fire upon us, I would rather not imagine her in a foul mood.
A legend widely circulated throughout the Near East tells of an ancient Corvosi king who succeeded in bringing a pari under his thrall. He had her craft magicked carpets for his own use, yet knowing the power at the pari's disposal, I struggle to imagine how the king would command her. During my research, I came across other folktales which illustrate elaborate rituals performed when making a pact with fantastic beings. One well-known example consists of three steps: first, one must convey peaceful intent through body language. Second, one must speak a specific set of words. Lastly, one must entertain the being as a final gesture of trust.
Somehow, I wager that the pari we encountered would fall asleep long before such an intricate rite was complete.

Name: Fragrant Fruits
Description:
*In a show of appreciation for his companion's bravery, the merchant plucked the sun-kissed fruit from a nearby tree and offered it to his bodyguard.*
The apples and grapes I sampled during our journey tasted much like the ones in Old Sharlayan, albeit with a slightly different texture. That is to be expected, of course, since most of those fruits are grown in Labyrinthos.
That climate of that grand repository is actually a recreation of the environment of southern Ilsabard, namely Corvos. Such conditions are ideal for both flora and fauna to thrive, and thus much of the fruit cultivated there derives from varieties originating in Corvos. However, in the intervening years since the illuminated manuscript was written, the fruits have undergone countless generations of selective breeding. This likely explains the familiar yet subtly different flavor.
One could say that what I ate within the book was something like the original stock of what we eat today. I have heard that crops grown in Corvos have changed drastically since the Garleans conquered the land, meaning such old varieties might even be lost to time. Given that the manuscript makes it possible to examine and even taste such rare specimens, it has a heretofore-unknown value as a historical source. I must share this discovery with an acquaintance of mine who studies agricultural sciences at the Studium.

Name: The Sunlit Expanse
Description:
*The rippling ocean surface and shells strewn upon the beach each reflected the sun's rays in a competition of brilliance.*
Every object that caught my eyes glittered like a gemstone, impressing upon me the beauty of the sea. We only beheld the scene in the daylight, but doubtless nightfall would have brought an even more wondrous sight. I have heard that in some areas along the coasts of the Corvos Narrow, the rocks are stained a pale pink, the color arising from a mixture of the environmental aether and the remains of small organisms accumulated over many long years. Again I am reminded how strange and fascinating nature can be.
Incidentally, among the several volumes of *The Merchant's Tale* I consulted was one from Radz-at-Han wherein the primary setting was a dazzling coastal region. In that edition of the story, the characters traversed a coral cave and walked inside a gigantic spiral shell. There, they accepted a starfish's invitation to the bottom of the sea, where the Sea King bestows upon them a pearl that glows with a soft pink light. This treasure was the one which ultimately moved the maiden's heart.
Indeed, corals, spiral shells, starfish, and such all made an appearance in our journey, yet none seemed to carry any special significance. Perhaps in another version of events these objects could play a more crucial role in the merchant's quest.

Name: Treasure at the Ocean's Floor
Description:
*Enshrined upon a pedestal of coral, the jewel flickered faintly. Any who looked upon it became entranced, as if swallowed by that strange light.*
The jewel dropped by the sea-maid shone with a mysterious light. The coral pedestal itself was also a sight to behold, colored with hues of white, blue, and pink, as if it housed the entirety of the sea and its creatures. Could it have been a pearl, I wonder? If so, it would have come from a clam of almost impossible size, although perhaps not so impossible in such nutrient-rich waters. I had a chance to examine the jewel and found it flawless. Clearly, even among the wonders of the ocean floor, it was a singular treasure.
Unfortunately, it was not enough. The magnificent jewel failed to move the maiden, bringing an end to our tale. I wonder what the merchant did after that. Did he offer his beloved the jewel even if she had no interest in it? Or did he, as the maiden suggested, sell it for unimaginable riches? Such a treasure would have fetched a fortune in trade. The fact that the maiden was unmoved makes me believe that what she seeks is neither beautiful, precious, nor rare.

Name: Ruler of the Radiant Sea
Description:
*The sea-maid sang a song of lament. And with every heart-wrenching verse, the merchant's chest tightened like a vise.*
Our final adversary fought as if weighed down by sorrow. Or perhaps I imagined it because the genie we faced prior was so cheerful by contrast. Even if her crestfallen expression was merely a contrivance of the story, I cannot help but wonder at the circumstances which caused it.
Stories and lore involving sea-folk are not limited to Corvos─they exist the world over. Some tales portray them as dangerous creatures who bewitch people and drag them into the depths, while others depict them as friends and allies. Taking this into account, perhaps the sea-maid in the manuscript might become more amiable depending on how events unfold. We could possibly even communicate and form a mutually beneficial relationship.
In our battle, it seemed the sea-maid controlled other sea creatures through song. This may imply her origins lie in a culture which held music in high regard. Even if we did not share a common tongue, a melody might convey our feelings properly. However, I fear neither my bodyguard nor I can sing loud enough to be heard from under the water's surface. Even so, there may be a way to make a sound that reaches even the ocean floor.

Name: The Ocean's Bounty
Description:
*The merchant apprised the shimmering waters before him, certain that they contained riches untold.*
While I tend to become distracted by grand scenery, even the most stoic soul would be stunned speechless by the beautiful sea we came upon. Beds of seaweed thick as a forest, glittering fish beyond counting, and crabs hoisting their claws up high as if rejoicing─truly, a culinarian would marvel at the ingredients gathered there. I daresay a truly skilled hand could prepare a full-course meal without stepping foot from those verdant shores.
Strangely enough, there was not a single fisher in sight, yet I suppose the sheer number of deadly creatures we encountered is sufficient explanation. Much like the protagonist of *The Merchant's Tale*, I am no fighter, so I was most grateful for my bodyguard's presence.
And although we met nary a soul, we noticed the plain markings of civilization along the way. Perhaps there was a fishing village we overlooked somehow. Oh, what I would not give for a glimpse into their way of life. Which of the sea's bounties did they favor? And how did they prepare them? I admit, the lack of answers does fill me with some measure of regret.

Name: Valley of Extremes
Description:
*That valley hid astonishing sights. Boulders drifted through the air like clouds, and tiny pebbles crashed to the ground like cannonballs.*
Among the many fantastic locales we have seen thus far, the valley has left the most indelible impression. There, magnetized rocks interact with the ambient aether of the environment, causing even the most massive to rise into the air like feathers on a breeze. However, should that delicate balance be interrupted even for a moment, the rocks overhead come crashing down at once. Truly, I have seen naught else like it.
In that most singular place in the valley's depths, we faced an equally singular foe─a lone swordswoman. She struck as soon as we approached her, as if she had been long awaiting a worthy opponent. In the Near East, a bow is a customary greeting, yet if we had followed polite custom, we would have been cut down in an instant.
After our return, my research indicated that long ago in Corvos, practitioners of an ancient sword art known as the Unyielding Blade would hone their skills deep within a similar valley. Anyone who set foot there would be compelled to fight, even if they were no warrior. If one could survive such deadly company for enough days and nights, they would be allowed to leave. Emerging from that place alive was indisputable proof of one's mastery of the blade. The lengths some go to achieve such heights are both impressive and terrifying in equal measure.

Name: A Blade Engraven
Description:
*Intricate patterns were carved into the blade, leaving no space untouched.*
We retrieved the swordmaster's weapon from the depths of the valley. The ornamentation alone marks it as a work of art, but the patterns carved into the blade itself are its most striking feature. The ancient technique of the Unyielding Blade passed down in Corvos is said to resemble other magic-focused sword schools, wherein the user channels aether into their weapon and unleashes it as an attack. Likely the patterns on the blade served to condense the wielder's aether, thus amplifying the power of such strikes.
This background explains why the valley became a training ground for aspiring swordmasters. The rocks there were so strongly magnetized that they floated in the air. By pouring lightning-aspected aether into them, one could strengthen the magnetism further. Conversely, earth-aspected aether could be used to weaken the effect. By honing one's mastery over the rocks in the valley, a prospective swordmaster could apply such knowledge to their deadly art.
In fact, we were able to observe several of the once-floating rocks strewn about the ground during our journey. I wonder what would happen if we infused them with aether somehow? At the very least, we would have a taste of the grueling training endured by the swordmasters.

Name: Master of the Unyielding Blade
Description:
*While the master of the Unyielding Blade wielded but a single sword, it cut from all directions at once.*
Our last foe was a practitioner of an ancient style of swordplay known as the Unyielding Blade. Never did I think I would behold such techniques with my own eyes, so the encounter was a pleasant but deadly surprise. In the real world, the swordmasters of the Unyielding Blade were systematically slaughtered in the war against the Garlean Empire. While some apparently survived this purge, they could not bear imperial oppression and rose up in a failed rebellion. At last, the final swordmaster was taken captive and met an unjust end, or so I have heard. Stories say that in ages past, the esoteric order would offer demonstrations of their skills as a show of loyalty to the royal family─yet another tradition that was stamped out under the imperial heel.
Recalling such displays reminds me of a scene in an edition of *The Merchant's Tale* written for children. In it, the characters reach a stage which shone in rainbow hues. It was built not for a king or queen, but as a ceremonial place to make an offering of friendship to a different race of people. If kings take pleasure in displays of martial prowess, what would pique the interest of an entirely different race? Gaining an understanding of the story's cultural background should allow us to anticipate the tale's twists before they happen, and use that insight to drive our actions while within the book.

Name: In a Harsh Nature
Description:
*Where the very earth disobeyed nature's rules, the merchant walked a fine line between life and death with every step.*
Life somehow finds a way to thrive in even the harshest environs. Although this is but my own theory, I think the struggle to survive makes both flora and fauna more delicious. I tested this theory on the mushrooms that clung to rocky surfaces of the valley walls, and I stand firm in its truth, for they were exquisite. Though they had the look and feel of hard stone, they melted in my mouth like soft cream. Blessed with both a striking texture and rich flavor, the mushrooms could serve as an aromatic accent in any number of dishes.
Curious as to whether such mushrooms existed in the real world, I researched an illustrated encyclopedia and found one that looked almost identical. Imagine my surprise when the description indicated strong hallucinatory effects. Perhaps because the world inside the book is an illusion to begin with, such effects were not reproduced. Regardless, I realized anew the danger of putting unfamiliar plant life in my mouth. I was cautious enough when traversing that valley of floating rocks─I should practice that same caution when next a strange new specimen rouses my appetite.

Name: The Eye of the Beholder
Description:
*It was the soot-covered plate which the maiden treasured above all.*
Almost buried in ash, the ruins standing by the sea were once home to the maiden who appears at the beginning of the story. It seems the people who lived there shared a type of fellowship with the sea-folk, using an altar to interact with them and exchange various goods. That was, until the day the Dandan appeared to threaten their peaceful lives. Stories tell of a brave young man who secluded himself in the mountains to hone his body and mind that he might fell the dread beast, but the Dandan did not wait. In time the village succumbed to its wrath. Bereft of both family and home, the young maiden drifted aimlessly until she reached the city where the book begins.
No matter how lustrous, no treasure could soothe such a troubled heart. Yet that soot-covered plate was enough to stir memories of the maiden's family, allowing her to face her past and turn her gaze to the future. Having risked life and limb to bring back something so precious, surely the young merchant became a trustworthy companion to the maiden.
Finding the item our clients request is indeed a gleaner's primary duty, yet at times it seems even the clients themselves are unaware of their true desire. To read between and beyond the client's words and find what they genuinely consider to be of “worth immeasurable” is our true task. That was what my father showed me, and I will keep that lesson close at heart as I continue my own journey.
